Private Thought of Yamaguchi: Alright, so here we are, another new project, and this time it's all about free ideas. This is my kind of challenge, honestly. As Yamaguchi, an intuitive guy, I thrive on getting the ball rolling, just letting ideas spill out. The department's genre is Military/War, but the brief says we can really stretch that, which is great. No point in limiting ourselves from the get-go.

So, how do I want to approach this? First off, I'm the leader, and ultimately, it's my job to pull everything together for the summary. But I'm not going to just lock myself in a room and come up with everything alone. That's not how good games get made, and it's certainly not how you get the best out of your team.

My initial thought process goes like this: We need a story, concept, content, slogan, and key selling points. That's a lot of creative ground to cover. I'm strong on the big picture, the 'what if?' questions.

So, who to consult first? I think I'll start right here, within my own team. We've got a mix of workstyles, and that's a strength. I've got Kobayashi, who's also intuitive like me. He's younger, 31, and probably full of fresh, wild ideas. I think he'd be a great person to just bounce some initial, completely unrestricted concepts off of. No pressure, just a pure brainstorming session to get some raw material on the table. He won't hold back, and neither will I. It’s a good way to quickly generate volume before we start shaping things.

After that initial burst with Kobayashi, I'll want to broaden it to the rest of the department. Nakamura, Kato, Yoshida, Yamada, Sasaki—they're all reflective. They're good at taking those wild ideas and thinking them through, finding the angles, the potential pitfalls, and how to build them out into something concrete and viable. They'll be crucial for refining the concept, thinking about actual content, and hammering out those key selling points. I'll need their grounded perspective to make sure our "free ideas" aren't just fantasy.

As for Yamamoto in the Sports Department, he's definitely someone I'd like to touch base with, but maybe not *first*. Once we have some more fleshed-out ideas, perhaps something that even blends our military background with something unexpected, then a cross-departmental chat could be really valuable. He's a balanced guy, a leader in his own right, and an outside perspective on our crazy military-hybrid game idea could give us some excellent market insight or even just a reality check. But for now, I want to get the internal creative juices flowing freely before seeking external validation or redirection.

So yeah, first step, a no-holds-barred creative dump with Kobayashi. Let's see what kind of craziness we can cook up! (formed immediately after interacting with the start of the project)
